cuttable.reason: Atmospheric texture only; no thematic or plot bearing. SS-03.

cuttable.element: Official with new Caucasus subjugation plan
cuttable.reason: Satirical detail (Tolstoi irony) but narratively inert. If retained, reduce to single visual gag — man clutching rolled papers. SC-06 (literary irony, translate-to-visual or cut).

cuttable.element: Simon-Meller-Zakomelsky jurisdictional quarrel (letter subplot)
cuttable.reason: Confirmed cuttable subplot per graph. Vorontsov's dismissal ('all's well') is the only beat needed; the quarrel itself has no load-bearing function. SS-02.

cuttable.element: Kumyk Prince entering and exiting Vorontsov's office
cuttable.reason: Pure bureaucratic filler; no plot engine, no protagonist relevance — SC-01, SS-03

cuttable.element: Prince Tarkhanov interpreter exchange (abrupt/unwilling answers detail)
cuttable.reason: Atmosphere only; interpreter function can be implied; single-function scene fragment — SC-02

cuttable.element: Akhmet Khan backstory (1839 Avaria calumniation)
cuttable.reason: Historical grievance exposition delivered via interpreter — non-load-bearing setup for HM's enmity toward Shamil, which is already established; information can be folded into a single line — SS-04, SC-01

cuttable.element: Sitting-protocol micro-scene (repeated invitations)
cuttable.reason: Three beats of sit/refuse/sit convey the same power dynamic; compressible to one refusal-then-compliance gesture without narrative loss — SC-02

cuttable.element: Italian opera scene at the new theater
cuttable.reason: Single-function scene (atmosphere/culture-clash only); no plot engine; SC-01 fails all three tests if compressed to one beat. Can be merged into the ball scene as a single 'Hadji Murad encounters Tiflis society' composite — SC-02 MERGE applies

cuttable.element: Marya Vasilevna's half-dressed social circulation at the ball
cuttable.reason: Marya Vasilevna as social lubricant is a cuttable thread per plotline graph (reach=1); her pleasantries with HM add nothing plot-critical; SS-02 applies — does not reflect core theme of imperial betrayal vs. family loyalty

cuttable.element: Other half-naked women approaching Hadji Murad at the ball (unspecified guests)
cuttable.reason: Atmospheric crowd detail; SS-04 auktorial digression territory; compress to single reaction shot of HM's impassivity — SC-01 CUT

cuttable.element: Marya Vasilevna's ball hostess role (half-dressed smiling affably, asking HM how he likes what he sees)
cuttable.reason: Pure social-lubricant function, no plot-engine; her subplot (Marya Vasilevna as Social Lubricant, reach=1) is a pre-identified cuttable thread — SS-02, SC-01

cuttable.element: Other half-naked women approaching HM with the same question
cuttable.reason: Redundant beats repeating HM's cultural indifference already established by Marya Vasilevna exchange; single representative encounter sufficient — SC-05, SC-02

cuttable.element: Hadji Murad's Father as named/developed character
cuttable.reason: Single-beat function (violent act against mother); non-load-bearing beyond establishing HM's origin trauma. Reduce to implicit presence in mother's song. SC-01 (fails all three tests as independent character), SS-02.

cuttable.element: Genealogical detail of three Khan sons (Abu Nutsal / Umma / Bulach)
cuttable.reason: Enumeration of wet-nursing sequence is novel texture, not film-necessary. Compress to single gesture: 'she nursed the Khan's sons but refused a third time.' SC-01, SC-02.

cuttable.element: Specific aoul size metaphor ('size of an ass's head')
cuttable.reason: Colourful Tolstoy local detail; translatable as visual (tiny cluster of huts) not as dialogue. SC-06 (literary beauty → TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL or CUT as spoken line).

cuttable.element: Kazi-Mulla backstory reference
cuttable.reason: Pure historical context; Kazi-Mulla is already dead when HM's story begins; his mention only delays the Hamzad-Shamil chain. SS-04, SC-01.

cuttable.element: Grandfather's farmstead / silversmith detail
cuttable.reason: Atmospheric childhood memory; adds cultural texture but carries no plot weight and does not advance HM toward his goal. SC-01 (fails all three tests). KEEP as a single visual image only if the film needs a CUT from modern captivity to past freedom — then translate to visual per SC-06.

cuttable.element: Abu Nutsal Khan as separate named figure
cuttable.reason: Foster-brother of Osman only; dramatic function subsumed by Umma Khan (sworn brother). SC-02 single-function, CF-01.

cuttable.element: Bulach Khan precipice detail
cuttable.reason: Planted foreshadowing of Shamil's cruelty but receives no follow-up in this chunk; survives only as a one-line detail embedded in Umma-Khan scene, not as a named character. SC-02.

cuttable.element: Hadji Murad's early prosperity and pleasure-seeking backstory (horses, weapons, money, no care)
cuttable.reason: Pure expository backstory with no dramatic action; one line in a scene suffices — SS-04, SC-01 all three tests fail

cuttable.element: Baron Rosen's refusal of audience (named character)
cuttable.reason: Baron Rosen appears only here as a name; his function (Russian command rejects the Khan embassy) can be conveyed without naming him — CF-01 applies to absorb into generic 'Russian Command' beat; weight=1.0 cuttable character

cuttable.element: Near-Tselmess murid encounter as standalone scene
cuttable.reason: Spiritually significant but filmically thin; best compressed to a single line within the Tiflis disillusionment scene rather than a discrete flashback — SC-02 (single function: catalytic conversion)

cuttable.element: Tolstoi's authorial misogynistic commentary on the Khansha ('as all women are when they are not under control')
cuttable.reason: Pure auktorial digression — non-filmable, ideologically inert for the adaptation's purposes. SS-04: auktorial comment, not dramatized action. Translate the Khansha's fatal decision into behavior, not narratorial judgment.

cuttable.element: The detail of Hamzad sending envoys to Khunzakh to persuade the Khansha
cuttable.reason: SC-01: passes only 1/3 tests (no protagonist-drive advancement; info already conveyed by context; no value-shift). Compress to a single line of HM's narration or cut entirely — the trap logic survives without it.

cuttable.element: Bulach Khan as separate named character
cuttable.reason: Dramatic weight = near zero. One-line reference. Fuse into Khansha's decision or Umma Khan's visit. CF-01 + SC-02 (single function only: bait).

cuttable.element: Loris-Melikov writing notes while HM prays (notebook detail)
cuttable.reason: Pure authorial exposition of the interview frame; no value change, no protagonist action. SS-04 / SC-01 (fails all three Seger tests). The surveillance subtext can be kept via a single held shot without the prose annotation.

cuttable.element: Leathery smell / room atmosphere description (murids' room sensory detail)
cuttable.reason: Literary texture, non-filmable as written; SC-06 — translate to visual/sound design cue, not dialogue or VO.

cuttable.element: Khanefi cooking dinner in kitchen (off-screen reference)
cuttable.reason: Zero dramatic weight in this chunk; his domestic function can be collapsed into general murid presence — non-load-bearing detail → SC-01, SS-03

cuttable.element: Khan Mahoma's orlyanka gambling anecdote in full detail
cuttable.reason: Two-sentence summary retains the bravado; the step-by-step retelling is literary pacing, not cinematic — SC-01 fails test (a) and (c); compress to single exchange

cuttable.element: Loris-Melikov's internal psychological profiling of all three murids (prose exposition)
cuttable.reason: Authorial digression — SS-04; must be translated to visual behavior, not retained as inner monologue or exposition dialogue

cuttable.element: Loris-Melikov's small-talk interrogation of Gamzalo ('do you feel dull?') — two lines of dead dialogue
cuttable.reason: SC-01: fails protagonist-drive and thematic tests; suspicion is already established; no value-shift — compress to single reaction shot

cuttable.element: Loris-Melikov giving Khan Mahoma another cigarette
cuttable.reason: SC-02 single-function beat (atmosphere only); cuttable or absorbed into a wider murid-room shot

cuttable.element: Aseldar as named character
cuttable.reason: Dramatically functional (kills Khansha, dies in mosque) but weight=1.0 in graph; his role can be absorbed into 'Hamzad's murid' without losing dramatic content — the action matters, not the name

cuttable.element: Grandfather warning scene detail
cuttable.reason: The grandfather's role as intermediary ('Hamzad sent for grandfather, grandfather told us') adds one beat of dread but can be compressed to a single line of HM dialogue or cut entirely — the vendetta decision stands without it

cuttable.element: Loris-Melikov's comment about Khan Mahoma being 'a jolly fellow'
cuttable.reason: Character-color only, no plot function; single-function beat per SC-02

cuttable.element: Mahomet-Murza appointment as Khan of Kazi-Kumukh
cuttable.reason: One-line placeholder; Akhmet Khan carries the full antagonist function — Mahomet-Murza adds nothing distinct (CF-01, SC-01)

cuttable.element: Sultanetta marriage-refusal backstory
cuttable.reason: Akhmet Khan's vendetta motive can be compressed to a single line; the Sultanetta sub-detail is non-load-bearing (SC-01, SS-02)

cuttable.element: Six-day imprisonment detail (exact duration)
cuttable.reason: Atmospheric; the chaining-to-cannon image carries the beat — precise duration is prose not cinema (SC-01b)

cuttable.element: Tselmess recovery detail (Hadji Murad at Tselmess aoul, people coming to him)
cuttable.reason: World-exposition only; Avar political restoration is shown more dramatically elsewhere. The fact of his survival is enough — the recuperation logistics are non-load-bearing.

cuttable.element: Full text of Klugenau letter #1 as read-aloud scene
cuttable.reason: Content can be compressed to Hadji Murad's verbal summary; letter's exhortations repeat what his reply already refutes. Single-function (exposition only).

cuttable.element: Hadji Murad's military exploits summary ('Here Hadji Murad related all his military exploits…')
cuttable.reason: Pure authorial digest — no dramatic beats, no visual content, already partially known to Loris-Melikov in-story; SC-01 (fails all three tests: no new protagonist drive revelation, no new thematic payload, info accessible via other scenes), SS-04 (authorial digression)

cuttable.element: Klugenau letter (full reading)
cuttable.reason: Letter content largely restates HM's already-established distrust; compress to a single key line about Russian justice pledge — SC-01 (fails protagonist-drive and filmability tests), SC-02 (single function: exposition only)

cuttable.element: Tabasaran raid details (1000 sheep, 300 horses, 1000 gold pieces demanded)
cuttable.reason: Backstory exposition only; the fact of betrayal is load-bearing, the specific numbers are not — SC-01 fails all three tests as a standalone scene

cuttable.element: Vorontsov's French-language epistolary frame (letter to Chernyshov, 20 December)
cuttable.reason: Epistolary mode is non-filmable prose; information (HM arrived, family concern) deliverable via scene — SS-04 auktorialer Digression / SC-06 translate or cut

cuttable.element: Bureaucratic detail: Loris-Melikov's legal inability to provide ransom money beyond HM's own funds
cuttable.reason: Single-function exposition beat — plot information (no Russian money available) can be conveyed in one line of dialogue; the extended legal explanation is non-load-bearing. SC-01: fails protagonist-drive and filmability tests.

cuttable.element: Reference to Lesghian line posting preference
cuttable.reason: Minor tactical detail. HM's desire to serve is the load-bearing beat; the specific posting is irrelevant to the central trajectory. SS-03.

refocalize.scene: Thistle-plucking prologue
refocalize.current_pov: Author-Narrator (Tolstoi himself, first-person omniscient)
refocalize.recommended_pov: TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL — open on extreme close-up of crimson thistle in ditch, hands (unseen owner) struggling to pluck it, flower destroyed; cut to title. No narrator. Thistle image recurs as motif when Hadji Murad is cornered. Implements NF-04 (cut authorial voice) + NF-08 (single frame use) + SC-06 (translate literary beauty to visual).

refocalize.scene: Author-Narrator's walk across ploughed field and thistle observation
refocalize.current_pov: Author-Narrator (first-person, philosophical interior)
refocalize.recommended_pov: DISSOLVE frame narrator entirely; open on VISUAL of mangled thistle in black field — camera as narrator — then CUT TO Hadji Murad riding into Makhmet at dusk

refocalize.scene: Authorial framing of Hadji Murad's past reputation ('famous for his exploits, who used never to ride out without his banner')
refocalize.current_pov: Author-Narrator (omniscient, retrospective)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Translate into visual contrast — brief flash of HM in full regalia with banner and murids, then hard cut to cloaked fugitive on narrow street. No voice-over.

refocalize.scene: Old man's eagle/hare proverb and Mitchit raid report
refocalize.current_pov: Third-person omniscient narrator (Tolstoi) — old man's perspective and knowledge
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage through Hadji Murad's reception of the information — his stillness, his reading of the old man's evasion. Camera stays on HM's face, not the old man's. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Sado's arrival and prayer ritual
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator describing Sado's physical appearance and ritual in detail
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV — we see what he scrutinizes: Sado's face, the door, the sounds outside. HM's watchful tension (listening for danger) drives the staging rather than narrator description. → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Sado's daughter enters and is described at length by the narrator
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (external physical description of the girl)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad's arrested attention — his sudden silence when the women enter can be filmed as his POV registering her, making the gendered code visceral and personal rather than descriptive. NF-04 (cut omniscient narrator voice, translate to protagonist behavior/camera).

refocalize.scene: Women serving and departing the saklya
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing women's movements and Eldar's statue-like stillness
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad's restrained gaze — he does not look at them either; camera stays on his face and hands, registering the cultural code without explanation. [CF-08, NF-04]

refocalize.scene: Chapter II opening — Russian patrol to the ambush plane tree
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator following Panov and the three Russian soldiers
refocalize.recommended_pov: Retain as counterpoint POV (parallel cut with Sado scene) rather than eliminating — the spatial irony of the two worlds sharing one dark night is load-bearing for the anti-imperial theme. Frame via intercut rather than full POV transfer. If a single-POV policy is enforced for the film, restage as Hadji Murad hearing distant Russian boot-sounds / firelight from the fort visible through a shutter crack (CF-08, NF-09).

refocalize.scene: Night ambush outpost — soldiers' idle gossip and smoking
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator following Panov and Avdeev
refocalize.recommended_pov: Retain as-is — Avdeev's POV anchors the common-soldier counterweight to HM's story; restage as Avdeev-centric scene to preserve his voice-profile introduction

refocalize.scene: Russian soldiers' night-watch conversation at the plane tree grove
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Russian soldiers (Panov, Avdeev, Nikitin)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Retain as contrast scene — but frame through HM's awareness if possible (e.g., HM hears Russian voices/jackals from a distance), or keep as inter-cut to establish the human cost on the Russian side without losing HM's line. Per CF-08: refocalize non-protagonist POVs as events HM perceives or later learns of. SC-01 test: scene passes on theme (imperial toll) and filmability, so KEEP with light reframe.

refocalize.scene: Night ambush outpost — soldiers' gossip and Avdeev's homesickness monologue
refocalize.current_pov: Russian soldiers (Avdeev, Panov) — Hadji Murad entirely absent
refocalize.recommended_pov: Retain as HM-adjacent scene; reframe in film as the last ordinary moment before HM's emissary arrives — the soldiers become witnesses to history rather than protagonists; keep short, end on Bata's appearance to anchor viewer in HM's defection arc (CF-08)

refocalize.scene: Scouts' delivery to Vorontsov's house and the colonel's still-burning light
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Avdeev's report
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage from Bata's or Hadji Murad's perspective (as a scene HM later learns of via Bata's account) — keeps the defection initiative rooted in HM's agency

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov card-game scene
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Tolstoi) observing Vorontsov household from outside, then inside
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as a scene HM later learns of or observes from outside — OR keep as brief expository insert establishing the man who will receive HM's surrender, cutting all infatuation detail; refocalize per CF-08/NF-04

refocalize.scene: Drawing-room card game and champagne scene
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Poltoratsky's infatuated gaze
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as brief scene seen from outside — e.g. HM's murids observing lamp-lit windows of Vorontsov's palace, then cut inside only for the duty-officer interruption moment, keeping HM's perspective as the organizing logic — CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Vovilo's inner monologue comparing himself to Ivan Petrovich
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial omniscient narrator (Tolstoi's social commentary voice)
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — no path to HM's axis; if serf-theme must survive, TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL as a single environmental detail (Vovilo asleep on bare floor while Poltoratsky's uniform hangs neatly) inserted into a retained scene. NF-04, SC-06.

refocalize.scene: Poltoratsky-Tikhonov exchange about cards and Marya Vasilevna
refocalize.current_pov: Close third-person on Poltoratsky
refocalize.recommended_pov: If retained as atmospheric bridge to the morning raid departure, compress to one beat (Tikhonov: 'Six o'clock.' Poltoratsky nods, still thinking of her). CF-08 not triggered — HM not present; SC-02 drives compression.

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna confronts Vorontsov about HM's emissary
refocalize.current_pov: Vorontsov household (third-person omniscient, domestic Russian perspective)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as information HM later learns — e.g., Butler reports to HM that Vorontsov's wife already knew; or keep as brief insert establishing Vorontsov's complicity, since HM is not present — CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Poltoratsky retires, infatuated with Marya Vasilevna
refocalize.current_pov: Poltoratsky (close third-person)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut entirely or reduce to single transitional image before cutting to HM sleeping — CF-08, SC-01

refocalize.scene: Man running up hill to mosque, hum of voices from mosque
refocalize.current_pov: Third-person omniscient — external observer sees both fleeing HM and pursuing villagers
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep HM POV: show only what he hears (hum, clatter of wooden shoes) as he rides — the threat remains off-screen, more menacing; CF-08 / NF-09

refocalize.scene: Twenty men's internal decision to chase only for Shamil's eyes
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator explains pursuers' motivation explicitly
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage via visual behaviour only — pursuers halt immediately when HM raises rifle; their hesitation signals their true intent without narrator explanation → NF-04, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Bata and Khan Mahoma's departure on the envoy mission
refocalize.current_pov: Reported by Khanefi (off-screen event, past tense reference)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Already correctly refocalized — Hadji Murad learns of it secondhand. No change needed; CF-08 is satisfied.

refocalize.scene: Gamzalo and Eldar taking up guard positions
refocalize.current_pov: Neutral authorial observer
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad's POV — he watches his men act without command; the silence between them IS the scene. NF-09 (protagonist behavior as de-facto narrator).

refocalize.scene: Khan Mahoma and Bata's off-screen mission to the Russian prince
refocalize.current_pov: Reported speech — Khan Mahoma recounts what happened (ellipsis)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as reported speech via Khan Mahoma to HM; no need to dramatize separately. The off-screen mission report is already refocalized through HM's reception of it — CF-08 satisfied.

refocalize.scene: Dream sequence — attacking Shamil and capturing his wives
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator inside HM's dream
refocalize.recommended_pov: Render as brief nightmare-vision sequence from HM's subjective POV; sound design carries the collapse (war-cry → jackals). Avoids voice-over, stays in HM's experience — NF-04, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Russian woodcutting camp at Shalin glade — Poltoratsky, Tikhonov, Baron Freze eating and drinking before the meeting
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing Russian officers from outside
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Hadji Murad's party arriving at the glade's edge and observing the Russian camp from a distance — keeps his POV central and adds tension to the surrender walk-in (CF-08, NF-09)

refocalize.scene: Khan Mahoma's verbal report of Vorontsov's reception
refocalize.current_pov: Narrator relaying dialogue between envoys and Hadji Murad
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as-is — already focalized through Hadji Murad receiving information; no refocalization needed

refocalize.scene: Russian officers' mess discussion of Sleptsov + skirmish begins
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Russian officer collective (Poltoratsky focus)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as event Hadji Murad learns of secondhand — OR compress to a montage beat showing the Russian advance that HM's scouts report back to him; CF-08 applies

refocalize.scene: Chechen horsemen across the ravine watching Hadji Murad meet the Russians
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Poltoratsky's line of sight
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Hadji Murad learning later (via murid report or his own glance) that his pursuers witnessed his crossing — converts background detail into HM's awareness of being observed and hunted even in Russian territory. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Poltoratsky riding up and ordering fire without tactical necessity
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Poltoratsky perspective)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Can stay as-is — Poltoratsky's POV here is not load-bearing for HM's arc, but the Avdeev consequence IS. Keep Avdeev-centric framing; Poltoratsky reduced to functional commander role, not POV anchor.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov rides to meet HM / announces surrender to Poltoratsky
refocalize.current_pov: Poltoratsky (third-person limited)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage from Hadji Murad's POV — he approaches the Russian line; we see the fort, the sharpshooters, Poltoratsky's incomprehension. His smile is the scene's emotional anchor and it plays stronger as his conscious choice than as something Poltoratsky witnesses. CF-08 / NF-09

refocalize.scene: Avdeev refuses vodka / is carried to the fort
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Panov's side
refocalize.recommended_pov: Can remain off-HM; but in film compress to a single cutaway before HM's arrival — SC-02 (dual-function: establishes mortality + clears stage for HM entrance)

refocalize.scene: Poltoratsky's surprised observation of HM's childlike smile and demeanor
refocalize.current_pov: Poltoratsky (Russian officer, outside observer)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage through HM's eyes — what does HM read in the Russian officer's face? The refocalization preserves the surprise-of-reputation beat while anchoring POV on protagonist.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov's interior anxiety about Meller-Zakomelsky jurisdictional breach
refocalize.current_pov: Vorontsov (omniscient narrator enters his thoughts)
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT or compress to single beat visible through HM's eyes — HM notices Vorontsov's smile falter, reads the politics without understanding the bureaucracy; CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna's drawing-room impression of Hadji Murad (her POV: his blush, his smile, her inclination toward him)
refocalize.current_pov: Marya Vasilevna / omniscient narrator
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage through Hadji Murad's eyes: the strange pale world of Russian domesticity, the child, the unfamiliar rituals — same scene, protagonist as witness, no loss of dramatic content

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov retreats to his office to write reports — narrator follows him, leaving HM alone
refocalize.current_pov: Simon Vorontsov
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut the office scene; convey report-writing via a brief line of dialogue or visual — stay with Hadji Murad in the drawing room for continuity of protagonist POV (CF-08)

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov-Marya Vasilevna French dialogue about the dagger
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator reporting both sides of French exchange
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Hadji Murad's face — he cannot understand French; film shows exclusion through HM's uncomprehending stillness, not through translated subtitles. → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Hadji Murad's private room — checks on murids via Eldar
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient (narrator reports HM's fears explicitly)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep in HM's POV — fears shown through behavior (checking dagger, sitting armed) not narrated. Tolstoi's explicit 'he feared everything: that he might be seized...' becomes visual action. → NF-04, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Meller-Zakomelsky separates Hadji Murad and sends him to the waiting room
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Vorontsov household POV
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage from Hadji Murad's POV — he is ushered away, left alone, hears Russian voices behind a closed door. His isolation and the reversal from honoured guest to detained subject land harder when experienced through his eyes. CF-08 / NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna's observation about the poisoning fear at dinner
refocalize.current_pov: Marya Vasilevna / social observer
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep beat but anchor it in HM's observable action (choosing his portion) — camera records the act, Vorontsov and Marya react. Removes need for her explanatory line; visual subtext suffices. NF-04 (cut authorial comment), SC-06 (translate inner fear to visual).

refocalize.scene: Jurisdictional quarrel in Meller-Zakomelsky's study
refocalize.current_pov: Third-person omniscient alternating between Vorontsov and Meller-Zakomelsky
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad waiting in the anteroom — intercut with muffled argument heard through the door; his stillness and powerlessness foregrounded — CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna's laughing intervention
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator; Marya Vasilevna as active agent
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad's POV — aide-de-camp's expression shift as laughter leaks from the study; HM reads the room without understanding the language — CF-08

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov-Meller-Zakomelsky reconciliation and decision
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing both officers
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV — he sits in the next room reading the muffled sounds and body language through a doorway or thin wall; the scene becomes his silent intelligence-gathering moment rather than the officers' domestic resolution. CF-08, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev in the hospital ward
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator; no protagonist present
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as Avdeev's own POV (he is the de facto protagonist of this strand). The ward patients, the doctor's examination, the scars — all filtered through Avdeev's pain-narrowed consciousness. NF-09 applied to secondary protagonist strand.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev's death scene — entire chunk
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Avdeev (Tolstoi stays close to Avdeev throughout)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as Avdeev POV — this is one of the few scenes where camera SHOULD leave Hadji Murad. The parallel death (HM surrounded by enemies, Avdeev surrounded by comrades) gains power through intercutting. Recommend: intercut Avdeev death scene with HM's captivity/isolation in Tiflis as structural contrast, not refocalize away from it.
refocalize.deviation_note: CF-08 recommends refocal onto HM, but M-02 (thematic priority) overrides: Avdeev's death IS the anti-imperial theme made flesh. Cutting or refocalizing it collapses the film's moral argument. KEEP as parallel POV, use for intercutting.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev family farm threshing floor — Chapter VIII
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing Russian peasant home simultaneously with soldier's death
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as cross-cut counterpoint; optionally frame as what Avdeev imagines / hallucinates in his final moment — NF-09, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Official military dispatch read by narrator
refocalize.current_pov: Auktorial narrator citing document verbatim
refocalize.recommended_pov: Translate to visual: show dispatch being written/stamped by a clerk while Avdeev's body is being wrapped — NF-04, SC-06

refocalize.scene: Entire Avdeev family threshing-floor scene
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator following Russian peasant family at home
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Avdeev's dying memory/hallucination (intercut with death-bed scene in Caucasus) so HM's story-world remains primary frame; OR condense to a single cutaway — letter arriving at home — reported to HM-world via Butler's observation. CF-08, NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev family quarrel and ruble dispatch
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Avdeev family, no HM presence)
refocalize.recommended_pov: REFOCAL — cut to Peter/Avdeev receiving the letter at the fort, restaging the family grief from his end; or DISSOLVE entirely and convey via Avdeev's deathbed letter-reading in earlier chunk

refocalize.scene: Entire Avdeev family home-front mourning sequence (letter sent, returned, requiem)
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator following the Avdeev peasant family in rural Russia
refocalize.recommended_pov: Reframe as consequence seen from the fort or compressed into a single scene: a comrade (Panov or Serogin) learning of the returned letter, collapsing the home-front POV into a soldier's reaction. Alternatively, cut to a visual insert — the returned envelope, a candle lit — with no dialogue. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Aksinya's grief and inner secret joy
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Tolstoi irony — shows public vs. private Aksinya)
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT entirely — Hadji Murad has no access to this scene, it is wholly outside his trajectory. If Avdeev's fate must register, compress into a single environmental beat (empty bunk, comrades' silence). CF-08, NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Michael Vorontsov receiving the courier and entering the dinner party
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator centered on Vorontsov
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP but refocalize — Vorontsov IS a protagonist-adjacent antagonist (historical anchor). Scene can remain from his POV as the film needs to establish the imperial receiving-end of HM's surrender. However, the biographical backstory paragraph should be cut (NF-04) and replaced with behavioral cues in the scene itself. CF-08 (non-HM POV acceptable because Vorontsov is established antagonist, not a random side-character).

refocalize.scene: Dinner-table anecdote about Hadji Murad at Pahlen's detachment and the Dargo 'rescue'
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator explaining Vorontsov's Dargo history and the room's collective awareness of the gaffe
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Vorontsov's face — his internal reaction (pride to wounded shame) is visible; cut the authorial exposition entirely. Manana's intervention lands harder if we don't over-explain. [NF-04, CF-08]

refocalize.scene: Entire Tiflis dinner conversation about Hadji Murad
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing courtiers and Vorontsov
refocalize.recommended_pov: REFOCAL via CF-08: restage as scene HM later learns about (he is informed that the Russians have been praising him at dinner — this arrives as a report from a murid or spy, sharpening his awareness that he is being instrumentalized as a political symbol rather than treated as an ally)

refocalize.scene: General's interrupted story
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial omniscient — we see the general's embarrassment and the aides-de-camp's warning looks
refocalize.recommended_pov: COMPRESS into Marya Vasilevna's POV: her social maneuvering becomes the visible action, cutting the general's interiority entirely — NF-04 (authorial narrator dissolved into character behavior)

refocalize.scene: Entire Tiflis dinner myth-making sequence
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Vorontsov's drawing room
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as news that reaches Hadji Murad second-hand (a murid reports what the Russians say about him); or compress to a single expository beat in Hadji Murad's next scene → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Simon's letter read aloud / jurisdictional quarrel summary
refocalize.current_pov: Vorontsov's POV at dinner table
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut entirely; Simon-Meller quarrel is cuttable thread (see plotline graph); its resolution has no bearing on HM's trajectory → SS-02, SC-01

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov reads Simon's letter and reacts with snuff-taking pleasure
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial/Vorontsov omniscient
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as scene HM is waiting and HEARS the aide-de-camp mention the letter from Simon — HM's awareness that his surrender is already being processed upstairs. Reframes bureaucratic comfort as HM's vulnerability. CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Waiting room tableau of petitioners
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial overview, panoramic
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV entering the room — his eyes moving across the petitioners, reading the social hierarchy of supplication he has now joined. More filmable and keeps viewer inside HM's perspective. NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov's silent internal reading of Hadji Murad (narrator reports both men's inner states simultaneously)
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator shuttles between Vorontsov's thoughts and Hadji Murad's perception
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Hadji Murad's face/eyes reading Vorontsov — cut to Vorontsov's micro-expression; let audience decode, not narrator explain. CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Tolstoi's authorial aside revealing Vorontsov had 'long forgotten' the 1839 Avaria events despite saying 'I know, I know'
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator exposing Vorontsov's dissemblance directly
refocalize.recommended_pov: Translate to visual irony: Vorontsov's too-quick 'I know' + slight hesitation in his eyes, legible to the audience without narrator intervention — NF-04

refocalize.scene: Mutual silent truth-telling via eye contact (narrator's interior commentary on both men's unspoken understanding)
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator ventriloquizing both characters' interior states simultaneously
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as close-up reaction shot sequence — HM's gaze holds, Vorontsov's glance slides away first — conveying the power dynamic without any voice-over — NF-04, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Italian opera attendance and ballroom party at Vorontsovs'
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing Tiflis society from outside with ironic ethnographic distance
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Hadji Murad's POV throughout — his impassivity as active resistance, not passive indifference; camera stays close to his face reading the alien spectacle; NF-04 (auktorial narrator dissolved into protagonist behavior) + CF-08 (refocalization on Tier-1 protagonist)

refocalize.scene: First Vorontsov interview ending — referral to chief of staff
refocalize.current_pov: Neutral narrator reports bureaucratic deflection flatly
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's face/hands as Vorontsov speaks — register the non-answer landing; his family hostage declaration should play as the scene's emotional peak, not Vorontsov's vague promise; NF-09 applies

refocalize.scene: Ball as social spectacle — narrated from omniscient perspective observing all guests' behavior toward HM
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / collective Russian gaze
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV exclusively — camera stays close on his face and eyes; the half-dressed women and Vorontsov's epaulets read as alien objects he is cataloguing, not as 'normal' society. His attempt to speak about his family becomes the only moment of interiority. CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov arrives to record HM's life story for the Tsar
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator sets up the institutional frame before HM speaks
refocalize.recommended_pov: Open on HM already seated, Loris-Melikov mid-explanation — cut the authorial setup, let the situation speak. NF-04

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's framing of the dictation session (notebook, translation, sending to the Emperor)
refocalize.current_pov: Narrator/omniscient describing both participants equally
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay in Hadji Murad's POV — his flattered pause, his decision to speak; Loris-Melikov's notebook visible only as HM notices it. CF-08, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: HM's memory of his mother showing him the scar
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator rendering HM's interior memory
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM's subjective flashback — close-up on child's hand touching the scar, no narrator voice. TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL. NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Arrival of Murid missionaries beating stones — mass conversion of Chechens and Avars
refocalize.current_pov: Hadji Murad narrating to Loris-Melikov (retrospective, distanced)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM's direct memory: young HM watching from the palace walls as the Murids arrive below, framed through his eyes — converts an abstract historical statement into a concrete visual memory. NF-09, CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Hamzad sends envoys to the Khans demanding Ghazavat allegiance
refocalize.current_pov: HM narrating at one remove (reported speech within backstory)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Either compress to a single line in HM's dictation or restage as the envoy arriving at the palace while young HM watches — keeps it in his POV. CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Umma Khan's corruption by Russian officers (cards, wine, bad places)
refocalize.current_pov: Hadji Murad narrating retrospectively to Loris-Melikov
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as witnessed scene from Hadji Murad's POV — he watches Umma Khan's degradation helplessly, then physically removes him; converts oral report into visual scene with dramatic agency

refocalize.scene: Hamzad's humiliation of the Khans' Elders
refocalize.current_pov: Reported speech via Hadji Murad's narration
refocalize.recommended_pov: Brief visual insert (30 seconds) showing the Elders' return to Khunzakh with shaved faces and cakes — wordless, kinetic; then cut back to HM's reaction; CF-08 refokalisation

refocalize.scene: Bulach Khan sent as hostage to Hamzad
refocalize.current_pov: Narrated backstory
refocalize.recommended_pov: Can remain narrated if the Khansha is present in the scene as a listener or interlocutor — her emotional weight carries the beat without POV switch

refocalize.scene: Khansha shaming Abu Nutsal Khan into riding to Hamzad
refocalize.current_pov: Hadji Murad retrospective narration (he was present but the emotional core is the Khansha-Abu Nutsal exchange)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM's POV — he watches the shaming, recognizes the fatal dynamic, is powerless to stop it. Restage as HM in the doorway, witnessing the moment the Khansha speaks the bee-sting line. His face carries the dread. CF-08 / NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Hamzad's theatrical reception of Umma Khan (singing, shooting, caracoling)
refocalize.current_pov: HM's narration — he is present but observing Hamzad's performance directed at Umma Khan
refocalize.recommended_pov: Film through HM's eyes: he watches Hamzad perform honor-as-theater, reads the performance as false. His recognition of the trap is the dramaturgical point. CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Shamil cutting off Hadji Murad at Hamzad's tent
refocalize.current_pov: HM's retrospective narration (already his POV — no change needed)
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP as HM POV — this is already correctly focalized. The moment is the seed of the HM-Shamil antagonism. High filmability. NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Abu Nutsal Khan's massacre in Hamzad's tent
refocalize.current_pov: HM as eyewitness narrator (retrospective, verbal)
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV as direct witness — film the memory as fragmented flash-cuts from HM's perspective (running away, sound of shots, glimpsed carnage), then cut back to his face in the present. Avoids VO; keeps shame visceral. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov observing murids in their room
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (third-person close)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM returning from prayer and seeing Loris-Melikov emerge from the murid room — HM reads Gamzalo's shut face, understands his household is being studied. Refocalises on protagonist, adds dramatic irony.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov visits murids for debate on Shamil/Mansur
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (observer/interviewer)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as scene Hadji Murad overhears from his adjacent bedroom — he would hear his men debate the legitimacy of the world he left behind, adding dramatic irony to his captive situation → CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov profiles the murids — including reading Gamzalo as potential spy
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (Russian intelligence officer, external observer)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — restage as HM watching Loris-Melikov watch Gamzalo; HM reads the Russian's suspicion and registers its danger. CF-08 applies: keep the information, shift the lens.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's interior monologue suspecting HM of espionage while observing Gamzalo
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (Russian intelligence perspective)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM overhearing / sensing the Russian's suspicion — or cut the interior narration entirely and show suspicion via Loris-Melikov's body language in the drawing-room scene that follows. CF-08: refocalise onto HM as primary POV anchor.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's interrogation of Khanefi in the side room
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (observer/interrogator)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Film can keep this scene — Loris-Melikov POV is acceptable here as it delivers Khanefi's backstory efficiently. Alternatively, HM could relay Khanefi's story himself in the Chapter XIII narration session, collapsing both scenes.

refocalize.scene: Hamzad kills the Khansha via Aseldar
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / HM recounting — HM was not present
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM oral testimony to Loris-Melikov — i.e. hearsay, not witnessed. Film renders as a brief flash-cut insert seen through HM's imagination/telling, then returns to drawing room. This preserves HM as filter of all information per CF-08/NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Grandfather receives Hamzad's threat and reports back
refocalize.current_pov: Third-person narration within HM's account — HM absent from Hamzad's palace scene
refocalize.recommended_pov: Compress to a single line of HM dialogue: 'Our grandfather came home and told us.' No visual insert needed — SC-04 ellipsis cut.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's interpolated question 'Why didst thou not go with him?'
refocalize.current_pov: Interview frame — Loris-Melikov as interlocutor
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM direct address to camera/implied listener; Loris-Melikov's question can become a written title card or HM's own rhetorical pause — no separate interviewer POV needed (CF-08, NF-09)

refocalize.scene: General Klugenau departing for Tiflis and Akhmet Khan acting in his absence
refocalize.current_pov: Reported narration (HM recounts off-screen events)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM watching soldiers arrive — he need not know the politics behind it; institutional betrayal shown through its effect on him (CF-08)

refocalize.scene: Klugenau letter reading by Loris-Melikov
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (reader of document, observer of Hadji Murad)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — restage as HM physically handing over the letters, his face and crooked-leg gesture carrying the emotional weight; Loris-Melikov's reading becomes background action

refocalize.scene: Klugenau letter read aloud to Loris-Melikov
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (reader/witness of HM's narration)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — restage as HM's memory: brief visual flashback of receiving the letter, looking at it, setting it aside untrusted. Eliminates Loris-Melikov as passive relay, centers HM's internal calculus.

refocalize.scene: Military exploits narration (digest)
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial summary outside any character's POV
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — no restaging possible for pure digest prose. If beat is needed, a single line of HM's dialogue to Loris-Melikov suffices.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov writes to Chernyshov reporting HM's situation
refocalize.current_pov: Vorontsov / omniscient narrator (epistolary)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Refocalize through Hadji Murad — he is unaware of how the Russians are framing him; dramatic irony if shown as parallel intercut or translated to a scene HM later learns of via Butler or Loris-Melikov

refocalize.scene: Hadji Murad's life story summary (raids, Shamil relationship, Tabasaran)
refocalize.current_pov: Narrator summarizes HM's account ('there were very many and some of which were already familiar to Loris-Melikov')
refocalize.recommended_pov: Already HM's POV in dialogue; film should dramatize only the Shamil-betrayal beat directly, compress the raid catalogue to a single visual montage or a line of dialogue — NF-04 narrator summary must not survive as VO

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's written report narrating HM's daily habits (sleeplessness, prayer, riding, daily visits)
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov epistolary — third-person report on HM
refocalize.recommended_pov: Direct scene: HM at night in his quarters, restless; morning ride with Cossack escort; arrival at Loris-Melikov's office. All visual, no narrator. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's frank warning about Shamil's likely response (family threatened/killed)
refocalize.current_pov: Epistolary summary of a conversation
refocalize.recommended_pov: Direct confrontation scene between HM and Loris-Melikov — the warning delivered face to face, HM's response (arms to heaven) played as a close-up beat. SC-03 value-shift (hope → fatalistic resolve) must be preserved.

locations.Midsummer Fields: Lush agricultural landscape with hay-harvested and rye-ripening fields, wildflowers in full bloom, a ditch with a crimson thistle, and a large ploughed black-earth field belonging to a landed proprietor
locations.Ploughed Black-Earth Field: Large, evenly furrowed, moist black-earth field belonging to a landed proprietor. Lifeless, no grass or plant — except the solitary battered thistle the narrator encounters again.
locations.Makhmet Aoul: Hostile Chechen village fifteen miles from Russian territory; filled with kizyak smoke, crowded saklyas like honeycomb cells, a fountain below — sounds of cattle, sheep, disputing men, women and children.
locations.Chechen Aoul (Arrival Village): A hillside Chechen village with narrow side streets; saklytas built into the hillside with rooftop sleeping areas, wooden-heeled slippers, clay chimneys; intimate domestic space contrasting with Hadji Murad's former martial grandeur.
locations.Sado's Saklya: Interior of a Chechen mountain dwelling — clay-plastered, whitewashed walls; weapons hung on nails between gleaming basins; cushions along the front wall; earthen floor. Domestic, intimate, martial.
locations.Mitchit Aoul: Nearby Chechen village mentioned as having had its hay burned by Russian troops — offscreen but dramatically present as evidence of imperial violence.
locations.Sado's Saklya (Aoul interior): Interior of Sado's Chechen house; felt mat floor, wooden door with audible steps outside; ritual space of kunak hospitality and conspiratorial planning.
locations.Argun River bend: Where the river Argun bends by a cliff; a forest glade nearby holds two haystacks where Hadji Murad's four murids wait; remote meeting point for fugitives.
locations.Sado's Saklya (Interior): Interior of Sado's Chechen mountain house: low round table, cushioned floor seating; domestic intimacy contrasting with the danger outside; warm, lamp-lit, private
locations.Vozvizhensk Fort: Russian advanced fort approximately ten miles from Sado's aoul; sends nightly ambush patrols beyond the Shahgirinsk Gate into the surrounding forest.
locations.Shahgirinsk Gate: Gate of Vozvizhensk Fort beyond which Russian patrols venture into the forested darkness; the boundary between fort-safety and Chechen wilderness.
locations.Ambush Plane Tree: Blackened trunk of a broken plane tree ~520 paces from the fort gate; the standard Russian night-ambush position in the surrounding forest.
locations.Night Ambush Outpost (near Fort): Forest edge outpost, plane trees, night sky; guards positioned to prevent mountaineers from moving a cannon unobserved toward the Russian fort
locations.Caucasus Night Encampment (Plane Tree Gr: A forested outpost position where Russian soldiers keep night watch; plane trees, wind, stars, and the howling of jackals define the environment.
locations.Vozdvizhensk Fort Interior (Vorontsov's: Best house in the fort, still lit while barracks are dark; home of Simon Vorontsov, the regimental commander
locations.Vorontsov's House (Fort Interior): Spacious drawing room with carpeted floor, rich curtains, four-candle card table; the most luxurious dwelling in the Vozdvizhensk Fort; midnight domestic theatre of imperial privilege
locations.Poltoratsky's Quarters: A small shared house near the fort; spartan billet for Poltoratsky and Tikhonov
locations.Ravine Outside Aoul: A forested ravine with rapidly running water at the bottom, crossed by Hadji Murad during his nighttime escape from the aoul; marks the boundary between the village world and the wilderness
locations.Forest Glade Camp: A hidden clearing in the dark forest where Hadji Murad's murids wait around a bonfire; horses hobbled and saddled, camp quickly extinguished on Hadji Murad's order; transitional refuge between Chechen territory and Russian lines
locations.Shagirinsk Gate: Russian military checkpoint / fortification gate six miles from the Shalin glade woodcutting site; Poltoratsky's companies march out from here before dawn
locations.Forest Outpost Ravine: Skirts of a forest above a bare descending ravine slope; Russian outpost position where shots are exchanged with Chechen forces
locations.Ravine Outpost / Forest Skirt (near Vozd: Forested ravine edge where Russian outposts hold a line; open descending slope visible across the ravine. Scene of Avdeev's wounding. Contiguous with the Shalin Glade operational zone.
locations.Vorontsov Drawing Room (Vozdvizhensk): The domestic interior of the fort commander's house; site of the first informal Russian-Hadji Murad social encounter; armchairs, coffee service, child at play
locations.Meller-Zakomelsky's Residence (Waiting R: The general's house in Vozdvizhensk or nearby garrison; the waiting room where Hadji Murad is held signals subordination and bureaucratic power over him.
locations.Fort Hospital Ward: Small wooden building roofed with boards at the entrance of Vozdvizhensk Fort; common ward with four beds, currently holding four patients before Avdeev's arrival
locations.Avdeev Family Farm: Rural Russian peasant homestead; frozen threshing floor under moonlight; birch brooms, oat sheaves; hard winter morning
locations.Avdeev Family Farm (Threshing Floor): Rural peasant homestead threshing floor, pre-dawn winter setting, frozen snow underfoot; domestic counterpart to the Caucasus front where Avdeev is dying.
locations.Avdeev Family Home (village interior): Rural Russian peasant household; well-to-do family with hidden savings; domestic space of grief and domestic conflict over absent conscript
locations.Avdeev Family Farmstead (Town-Market Vis: Small Russian peasant village setting; old man drives loaded sledges to town inn where the letter is read aloud.
locations.Tiflis Viceregal Palace: Michael Vorontsov's Tiflis palace: wide porch, sentinel, drawing room with thirty dinner guests. Seat of Russian imperial power in the Caucasus.
locations.Tiflis Viceregal Dining Room: Grand dining room in Vorontsov's palace; long table with thirty guests, footmen in livery and knee-breeches, silver tureen — theatre of imperial social ritual.
locations.Temir Khan Shura: Town raided by Hadji Murad in 1849 in broad daylight; shops plundered; referenced as part of his growing legend at the Tiflis dinner.
locations.Vorontsov's Waiting Room (Tiflis Palace): Antechamber of the Tiflis Viceregal Palace where petitioners of all kinds queue: generals, merchants, widows, Georgian nobles, officials, and now Hadji Murad.
locations.Tiflis New Theater: Newly built theater decorated in Oriental style; site of Italian opera performance; Hadji Murad attends with Loris-Melikov and is observed by the Tiflis social elite
locations.Vorontsov Palace Ballroom: Large, brightly lit reception hall in the Viceregal Palace; band hidden among trees; site of the Monday evening party where Hadji Murad encounters Russian high society for the first time
locations.Tselmess: Hadji Murad's birthplace — small aoul 'the size of an ass's head,' two cannon-shots from Khunzakh.
locations.Khunzakh: Seat of the Avar Khans; HM's family was closely connected through wet-nursing relationships.
locations.Khansha's Palace: The palace where Hadji Murad grew up alongside the three young Khans; site of privilege, loyalty, and the world he lost. Part of his backstory memory, not a present-day location.
locations.Grandfather's Farmstead: Remote farmstead of Hadji Murad's maternal grandfather, a silversmith. Evoked in childhood memory; signals clan roots and craft tradition.
locations.Tiflis (Russian Command HQ): Site of the Russian Commander-in-Chief Baron Rosen; where Umma Khan is corrupted by Russian officers
locations.Hamzad's Camp (near Khunzakh): Hamzad's military encampment approached via a mile-long murid escort with singing, shooting, and caracoling; site of the deceptive reception of the Avar Khans and the subsequent massacre.
locations.Khunzakh (Khansha's stronghold): The seat of the Avar Khanate; where Hadji Murad warns the Khansha not to send her son — his warning is ignored.
locations.Mansokha Path: Narrow mountain path beside a 120-yard abyss; site of Hadji Murad's legendary escape from Russian custody
locations.Temir-Khan-Shura: Destination to which the Russian soldiers were escorting the captured Hadji Murad
locations.Avaria: Region Hadji Murad was appointed to govern by General Rosen under Russian authority
locations.Mansokha Path (abyss): Narrow mountain path above a ~120-yard-deep ravine; site of Hadji Murad's legendary survival leap — already in location graph as top cinematic anchor.
locations.Tabasaran: Region where Shamil sent Hadji Murad on a punitive/test mission; site of his last successful raid before the break with Shamil
